question: What is the maximum air ambulance travel distance covered under the policy?
vectorRAG_answer: The maximum air ambulance travel distance covered under the policy is 150 kilometers. If the distance traveled is more than 150 kilometers, a proportionate amount of expenses up to 150 kilometers shall be payable. [Page 1]
graphRAG_answer: The maximum air ambulance travel distance covered under the policy is "150 kms." This information is derived from the relationship: (expenses [COST]) —[Payable Up To]? (150 kms [DISTANCE]) [Page 1].
hybridRAG_answer: The maximum air ambulance travel distance covered under the policy is 150 kilometers. If the distance exceeds 150 kilometers, only expenses up to 150 kilometers will be covered.
vectorRAG_sources:
[{"text": "the subject matter of solicitation. 
 
 
 
 
Air Ambulance Cover  
 
In consideration of the payment of additional premium to Us, We will cover the expenses incurred on air 
ambulance services in respect of an Insured Person which are offered by a healthcare or an air 
ambulance service provider and which have been used during the Policy Period to transfer the Insured 
Person to a Hospital or transfer from one hospital to another with adequate emergency facilities for the 
provision of Emergency Care, provided that:  
i. 
Our maximum liability under this Benefit for any and all claims arising during the Policy 
Year will be restricted to the Sum insured as stated in the Policy Schedule;  
ii. 
The maximum distance of travel undertaken is 150 kms. In case of distance travelled is 
more than 150 kms, proportionate amount of expenses upto 150 kms shall be payable. 
 
Example: If insured has travelled a distance of 300kms using an air ambulance we will pay", "metadata": {"source_file": "ceb7c087eb8c.pdf", "page_number": 1, "chunk_index": 1, "page_chunk_index": 1}, "score": 0.7184185981750488}, {"text": "more than 150 kms, proportionate amount of expenses upto 150 kms shall be payable. 
 
Example: If insured has travelled a distance of 300kms using an air ambulance we will pay 
50% of the total cost or Sum Insured whichever is lower. (Eligibility/Actual distance travelled 
: 150kms/300kms = 0.5) 
 
iii. 
It is for a life threatening emergency health condition/s of the Insured Person which requires 
immediate and rapid ambulance transportation from the place where the Insured Person is 
situated at the time of requiring Emergency Care to a hospital provided that the 
transportation is for Medically Necessary Treatment, is certified in writing by a Medical 
Practitioner, and road ambulance services cannot be provided.  
iv. 
Such air ambulance providing the services, should be duly licensed to operate as such by a 
competent government Authority.  
v. 
This cover is limited to transportation from the area of emergency to a  Hospital providing", "metadata": {"source_file": "ceb7c087eb8c.pdf", "page_number": 1, "chunk_index": 2, "page_chunk_index": 2}, "score": 0.7123141288757324}, {"text": "competent government Authority.  
v. 
This cover is limited to transportation from the area of emergency to a  Hospital providing 
emergency care which is not available at the place of origin and from one medical centre to 
another. ;  
We will not cover:  
a. Transfer of the Insured Person from one medical facility to another medical facility of 
similar capabilities which provides a similar level of care. 
b. Any transportation of the Insured Person from Hospital to the Insured Person’s 
residence after he/she has been discharged from the Hospital  
c. Any transportation or air ambulance expenses incurred outside the geographical scope 
of India.  
d. Attempt at suicide 
e. Injuries resulting from participation in acts of war or insurrection 
f. commission of unlawful acts 
g. Incidents involving use of drugs unless prescribed by a medical practitioner 
h. h. We have accepted a claim under Section II.A.1 in respect of the Insured Person for the

i.

question: What expenses are included under routine medical care for Well Mother cover?
vectorRAG_answer: The expenses included under routine medical care for the Well Mother cover are expenses recommended by a doctor and incurred on Pharmacy, Diagnostics, Doctor Consultations, and Therapy [Page 2].
graphRAG_answer:
The expenses included under routine medical care for Well Mother cover are:

1. Pharmacy, Diagnostics, Doctor Consultations, and Therapy.
   - Reference: (Routine Medical Care [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Includes Expenses On]? (Pharmacy, Diagnostics, Doctor Consultations and Therapy [EXPENSE CATEGORIES]) [Triplet 4]

2. Routine Preventive Care Services, which further includes expenses on Pharmacy and Diagnostic Tests.
   - Reference: 
     - (Routine Medical Care [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Includes]? (Routine Preventive Care Services [MEDICAL SERVICE]) [Triplet 5]
     - (Routine Preventive Care Services [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Includes Expenses On]? (Pharmacy and Diagnostic Tests [EXPENSE CATEGORIES]) [Triplet 10]

3. Customary Examinations.
   - Reference: (Routine Medical Care [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Includes]? (Customary Examinations [SERVICE COMPONENT]) [Triplet 7]

4. Immunizations.
   - Reference: (Routine Medical Care [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Includes]? (Immunizations [SERVICE COMPONENT]) [Triplet 8]
hybridRAG_answer: The expenses included under routine medical care for Well Mother cover are those recommended by a doctor and incurred on pharmacy, diagnostics, doctor consultations, therapy, routine preventive care services (including pharmacy and diagnostic tests), customary examinations, and immunizations.

question: What preventive care services are covered for a newborn baby?
vectorRAG_answer: The preventive care services covered for a newborn baby include routine preventive care services and immunizations within the hospitalization period. This coverage is part of the routine medical care provided to a newborn baby, which includes appropriate customary examinations required to assess the integrity and basic functions of the child's organs and skeletal structure carried out immediately following birth [Page 2].
graphRAG_answer:
The preventive care services covered for a newborn baby include "Routine Preventive Care Services," which encompass expenses on "Pharmacy and Diagnostic Tests." This information is derived from the following relationships in the knowledge graph:

- (Routine Preventive Care Services [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Include Expenses On]? (Pharmacy and Diagnostic Tests [EXPENSE CATEGORIES]) [Triplet 30]
- (Routine Medical Care [MEDICAL SERVICE]) —[Provided To]? (New Born Baby [ENTITY]) [Triplet 29]
hybridRAG_answer: The preventive care services covered for a newborn baby include routine preventive care services and immunizations during the hospitalization period. This care involves necessary examinations to assess the baby's organ and skeletal health immediately after birth.

question: Is infertility treatment covered under the Well Mother cover?
vectorRAG_answer: No, infertility treatment is not covered under the Well Mother cover. This is explicitly stated in the document: "We will not cover 1. Any infertility treatments" [Page 2].
graphRAG_answer: The Well Mother cover does not cover infertility treatments. This is supported by the triplet: (infertility treatments [MEDICAL_TREATMENT]) —[Does Not Cover]? (policy [INSURANCE_POLICY]) [source_page=2, source_chunk=7, confidence=0.95].

question: What is the mode of payment for air ambulance claims?
vectorRAG_answer: The mode of payment for air ambulance claims is by reimbursement only. [Page 1]
graphRAG_answer: The mode of payment for air ambulance claims is "Reimbursement." This information is derived from the triplet: (Air Ambulance expenses [EXPENSE]) —[Payable By]? (Reimbursement [PAYMENT_METHOD]) [Page 1].
hybridRAG_answer: The mode of payment for air ambulance claims is by reimbursement only.

Air Ambulance Cover  
 
In consideration of the payment of additional premium to Us, We will cover the expenses incurred on air 
ambulance services in respect of an Insured Person which are offered by a healthcare or an air 
ambulance service provider and which have been used during the Policy Period to transfer the Insured 
Person to a Hospital or transfer from one hospital to another with adequate emergency facilities for the 
provision of Emergency Care, provided that:  
i. 
Our maximum liability under this Benefit for any and all claims arising during the Policy 
Year will be restricted to the Sum insured as stated in the Policy Schedule;  
ii. 
The maximum distance of travel undertaken is 150 kms. In case of distance travelled is 
more than 150 kms, proportionate amount of expenses upto 150 kms shall be payable. 
 
Example: If insured has travelled a distance of 300kms using an air ambulance we will pay", "metadata": {"source_file": "ceb7c087eb8c.pdf", "page_number": 1, "chunk_index": 1, "page_chunk_index": 1}, "score": 0.5637096166610718}, {"text": "more than 150 kms, proportionate amount of expenses upto 150 kms shall be payable. 
 
Example: If insured has travelled a distance of 300kms using an air ambulance we will pay 
50% of the total cost or Sum Insured whichever is lower. (Eligibility/Actual distance travelled 
: 150kms/300kms = 0.5) 
 
iii. 
It is for a life threatening emergency health condition/s of the Insured Person which requires 
immediate and rapid ambulance transportation from the place where the Insured Person is 
situated at the time of requiring Emergency Care to a hospital provided that the 
transportation is for Medically Necessary Treatment, is certified in writing by a Medical 
Practitioner, and road ambulance services cannot be provided.  
iv. 
Such air ambulance providing the services, should be duly licensed to operate as such by a 
competent government Authority.  
v.

This cover is limited to transportation from the area of emergency to a  Hospital providing

question: If an insured person travels 300 km using an air ambulance, how much reimbursement will be paid?
vectorRAG_answer: If an insured person travels 300 km using an air ambulance, 50% of the total cost or the Sum Insured, whichever is lower, will be reimbursed. This is because the maximum distance covered for reimbursement is 150 km, and if the distance traveled is more than 150 km, a proportionate amount of expenses up to 150 km is payable. [Page 1]
graphRAG_answer:
To determine the reimbursement for an insured person traveling 300 km using an air ambulance, we need to refer to the relevant triplets in the knowledge graph:

1. (maximum distance of travel [DISTANCE]) —[Is]? (150 kms [DISTANCE]) [Triplet 2]
2. (expenses [COST]) —[Payable Up To]? (150 kms [DISTANCE]) [Triplet 23]

From these triplets, we can see that the maximum distance covered for reimbursement is 150 kms. Since the insured person traveled 300 km, which exceeds the maximum reimbursable distance of 150 kms, the reimbursement will only cover up to 150 kms.

Therefore, the reimbursement will be limited to the expenses incurred for 150 kms of travel.

The exact reimbursement amount is not specified in the knowledge graph, only the distance limit is provided.

question: What happens if the air ambulance distance exceeds 150 km?
vectorRAG_answer: If the air ambulance distance exceeds 150 km, a proportionate amount of expenses up to 150 km shall be payable. For example, if the insured has traveled a distance of 300 km using an air ambulance, the insurer will pay 50% of the total cost or the Sum Insured, whichever is lower. This is calculated as (Eligibility/Actual distance traveled: 150 km/300 km = 0.5) [Page 1].

question: Under what conditions will air ambulance services be approved?
vectorRAG_answer:
Air ambulance services will be approved under the following conditions:

1. It is for a life-threatening emergency health condition of the Insured Person that requires immediate and rapid ambulance transportation to a hospital for Medically Necessary Treatment, certified in writing by a Medical Practitioner, and when road ambulance services cannot be provided [Page 1, Passage 1].

2. The air ambulance service must be duly licensed to operate by a competent government authority [Page 1, Passage 1].

3. The maximum distance of travel undertaken is 150 kms. If the distance traveled is more than 150 kms, a proportionate amount of expenses up to 150 kms shall be payable [Page 1, Passage 2].

4. The transportation is limited to moving the Insured Person from the area of emergency to a hospital providing emergency care that is not available at the place of origin, or from one medical center to another [Page 1, Passage 4].

5. Claims towards air ambulance expenses will be payable by mode of reimbursement only [Page 1, Passage 3].
graphRAG_answer:
Air ambulance services will be approved under the following conditions:

1. The air ambulance must be licensed by a competent government authority. [Triplet 3]
2. The insured person requires ambulance transportation to a hospital. [Triplet 24]
3. The expenses for air ambulance services are payable by reimbursement. [Triplet 12]
4. The air ambulance cover includes expenses and has a maximum liability up to the sum insured. [Triplets 21, 22]
5. The maximum distance of travel for air ambulance services is limited to 150 kms. [Triplets 2, 23]
hybridRAG_answer: Air ambulance services will be approved if they are for a life-threatening emergency requiring immediate transport to a hospital, certified by a medical practitioner, and when road ambulance services are not possible. The air ambulance must be licensed by a competent authority, and the maximum travel distance is 150 kms, with expenses payable by reimbursement. The service is for moving the insured person to a hospital providing necessary emergency care not available at the origin.

ground_truth: Air ambulance services will be approved only for life-threatening emergency conditions requiring immediate transportation for medically necessary treatment, certified by a medical practitioner, where road ambulance services cannot be provided.

question: List all exclusions under the Air Ambulance Cover.
vectorRAG_answer:
The exclusions under the Air Ambulance Cover are as follows:

1. Transfer of the Insured Person from one medical facility to another medical facility of similar capabilities which provides a similar level of care.
2. Any transportation of the Insured Person from Hospital to the Insured Person’s residence after he/she has been discharged from the Hospital.
3. Any transportation or air ambulance expenses incurred outside the geographical scope of India.
4. Attempt at suicide.
5. Injuries resulting from participation in acts of war or insurrection.
6. Commission of unlawful acts.
7. Incidents involving use of drugs unless prescribed by a medical practitioner.
8. If Medically Necessary Treatment can be provided at the Hospital where the Insured Person is situated at the time of requiring Emergency Care. [Page 1]

ambulance services in respect of an Insured Person which are offered by a healthcare or an air 
ambulance service provider and which have been used during the Policy Period to transfer the Insured 
Person to a Hospital or transfer from one hospital to another with adequate emergency facilities for the 
provision of Emergency Care, provided that:

Year will be restricted to the Sum insured as stated in the Policy Schedule;

question: What are the available coverage periods under the Well Mother cover?
vectorRAG_answer:
The available coverage periods under the Well Mother cover are:

i) At the onset of pregnancy and up to pre-hospitalization period for maternity.
ii) At the onset of pregnancy and maternity hospitalization (only routine preventive care services and immunizations) up to first discharge from hospital.
iii) At the onset of pregnancy, maternity hospitalization (only routine preventive care services and immunizations) and until 30 days following birth of newborn baby. 

[Page 2]

hybridRAG_answer: The available coverage periods under the Well Mother cover are: from the onset of pregnancy up to the pre-hospitalization period for maternity; from the onset of pregnancy through maternity hospitalization (covering only routine preventive care services and immunizations) up to the first discharge from the hospital; and from the onset of pregnancy through maternity hospitalization (covering only routine preventive care services and immunizations) until 30 days following the birth of the newborn baby.

question: Does the policy cover transportation from hospital to home after discharge?
vectorRAG_answer: The policy does not cover transportation from the hospital to the insured person's residence after discharge. This is explicitly stated in the context: "We will not cover: ... b. Any transportation of the Insured Person from Hospital to the Insured Person’s residence after he/she has been discharged from the Hospital" [Page 1].
graphRAG_answer: The policy does not cover transportation from hospital to home after discharge. This is explicitly stated in the knowledge graph triplet: (Cover [INSURANCE COVERAGE]) —[Excludes]? (Transportation from Hospital to residence after discharge [EXCLUSION]) [Page 1].
hybridRAG_answer: The policy does not cover transportation from the hospital to home after discharge.

question: Are unlawful acts covered under air ambulance claims?

graphRAG_answer: Unlawful acts are not covered under air ambulance claims. This is indicated by the triplet: (Cover [INSURANCE COVERAGE]) —[Excludes]? (Commission of unlawful acts [EXCLUSION]) [Page 1].

Air Ambulance Cover

transportation is for Medically Necessary Treatment, is certified in writing by a Medical

question: My newborn required diagnostic tests immediately after birth. Will these expenses be covered?
vectorRAG_answer: Yes, the expenses for diagnostic tests required immediately after birth for a newborn are covered. Routine medical care for a newborn includes expenses recommended by a doctor and incurred on pharmacy and diagnostic tests, as specified in the document [Page 2].
graphRAG_answer: Yes, the expenses for diagnostic tests for your newborn will be covered. According to the knowledge graph, "Routine Medical Care" is provided to a "New Born Baby" and includes expenses on "Pharmacy and Diagnostic Tests" ([29], [15]).
hybridRAG_answer: Yes, the expenses for diagnostic tests required immediately after birth for a newborn are covered as part of routine medical care.

Air Ambulance Cover  
 
In consideration of the payment of additional premium to Us, We will cover the expenses incurred on air 
ambulance services in respect of an Insured Person which are offered by a healthcare or an air 
ambulance service provider and which have been used during the Policy Period to transfer the Insured 
Person to a Hospital or transfer from one hospital to another with adequate emergency facilities for the 
provision of Emergency Care, provided that:  
i. 
Our maximum liability under this Benefit for any and all claims arising during the Policy 
Year will be restricted to the Sum insured as stated in the Policy Schedule;  
ii. 
The maximum distance of travel undertaken is 150 kms. In case of distance travelled is 
more than 150 kms, proportionate amount of expenses upto 150 kms shall be payable.
